Reaction No. 54431
H+1 + S-2 = H+1_S-2

Note: The S-2(aq) species does not exist; pKas have changed erratically over time and
are now well into the region where they have no significance. Raman measurements by
us [18MBH_30000] show that the only previous data should not be considered as a
deprotonation of H+1_S-2 to form S-2 but rather a significant interaction with Na+
(c.f. Cs+) with increasing [NaOH]. Changes in pKa have introduced troubling
thermodynamic inconsistencies in the literature. Eradicating S-2 information content
from the database is thus necessary.

Reaction No. 54775
Ca+2 + SO4-2 + 2<H2O> = Ca+2_SO4-2_H2O(2)_(s)

Note: This is a classic case where medium effects are critical at moderate or high
ionic strengths. Note the differences even between chloride and perchlorate mediums,
given that the constants have been measured by highly reputable laboratories and are
considered accurate. The impressive study by Marshall and Slusher [66MaS_8399]
dominates the available literature data, as reflected below. However, accurate
predictions of gypsum solubility are not possible at the current state of the art
(unless simplicity is assumed and parameterised!)

Reaction No. 54982
2<O2(g)> + S(s) + 2<e-1> = SO4-2

Note: From Wolery & Colon [17Woc_29520], p. 654 footnote to Table 5: "USGS 1259 gives
the Gibbs energy of formation of SO4-2 as 117.970 kcal/mol. This is a typographical
error. The intended value (from the cited source, NBS 270-1) was 177.97 kcal/mol.
This becomes 744.529 kJ/mol at 1 bar." This is a critical reaction. Knock-on effects
of the older values are being slowly superseded. However, they cannot just be
abandoned too quickly because this produces bad inconsistencies elsewhere, esp. re
BaSO4(s), which corrupt S(s)/SO4-2 equilibria more problematically. I guess things
will eventually end up closer to lgK0 = 130.0

                                   Data Assessment



                                       – Page 684 of 731 –
JESS Thermodynamic Database v8.9                                Sulphur



Weight                       Assessment
9        highest accuracy
8        multi-determined; excellent dependability
7        trustworthy; outstanding single determination
6        better than average reliability
5        average single determination
4        some doubt
3        poorly known
2        guess to achieve consistency (or just as bad)
1        no information but consistent
0        problematic/inconsistent




                                          Techniques


Abbrev                      Method
CMN      Several experimental values (crit.)
CRV      Critical review
CSM      Smith & Martell Vols 1-6 (crit.)
EAE      Automated extrapolation
EBP      By Proxy
EBU      Brown's Unified Theory
EDH      Debye-Hueckel corrections
EDT      Debye-Hueckel & Temperature corrections
EEC      Entropy correspondence principle
EES      Personal estimate
EFE      Free Energy relations
ELC      Linear Combination of Reactions
EMS      Mean Spherical Approximation
EMU      Method unknown
ENS      Not specified
EOD      Calculated from data of others
EOR      Other Reaction Constants
EOS      Optimised Single Species Param.




                                          – Page 685 of 731 –
JESS Thermodynamic Database v8.9                           Sulphur


EPZ    Pitzer model correction
ERG    Relative Gibbs energy
ERP    Regression procedure
ERS    Rough subjective
ESC    Standard conditions anchor
ESI    Specific Ion Interaction Theory
ESR    Similar reaction(s)
ETM    Temperature corrections
ETR    Thermodynamic relationships
ETS    Ternary (statistical)
EVH    Van't Hoff Equation (dH = constant)
EVK    Variation of lgK with T
MAC    Activity coefficient (not specified)
MAG    Silver electrode e.m.f.
MAM    Amperometry
MAX    Anion exchange
MCE    Cation exchange
MCG    Chromatogaphy, Gel
MCL    Calorimetry
MCN    Conductivity
MCO    Colorimetry (often for measuring pH)
MCU    Copper electrode e.m.f.
MCV    Cyclic voltammetry
MDG    Combination of Thermodynamic Data
MDR    Dielectric Relaxation Spectroscopy
MDS    Distribution between two phases
MDV    Dilatometry, densimetry
MEF    Electromotoric force, not specified
MEP    Electrophoresis
MES    Electron spin resonance
MFP    Freezing point
MGL    Glass electrode
MHG    Emf with amalgam electrode
MHY    Emf with hydrogen electrode
MIE    Current-voltage studies
MIS    Ion selective electrode

MQH    Quinhydrone electrode
